content: Python is a high-level programming language known for its readability. It uses indentation to define blocks of code. Variables in Python are dynamically typed.
A computer network is a collection of interconnected devices that can communicate and share resources. The OSI model has 7 layers, with the Physical layer at the bottom.
Machine Learning is a subset of AI that allows systems to learn from data without explicit programming. Supervised learning involves training on labeled data.
Cybersecurity involves protecting computer systems and networks from information disclosure, theft of or damage to their hardware, software, or electronic data.
Web Development involves building and maintaining websites. It consists of frontend (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) and backend (server, database) technologies.
Full Stack Development covers both client-side and server-side engineering, bridging UI design, business logic, API integrations, and database management.
Data Science integrates domain expertise, programming skills, and knowledge of mathematics and statistics to extract meaningful insights from data.
Cloud Computing refers to the on-demand delivery of IT resources and applications over the Internet with pay-as-you-go pricing, instead of buying and operating physical servers.
DevOps is a set of practices that combines software development (Dev) and IT operations (Ops) to shorten the systems development life cycle and provide continuous high-quality delivery.
Blockchain is a decentralized, distributed ledger technology that securely records transactions across a peer-to-peer network without needing a central authority.
The Internet of Things (IoT) describes the network of physical objects embedded with sensors, software, and other technologies for the purpose of connecting and exchanging data.
An Embedded System is a computer system with a dedicated function within a larger mechanical or electrical system, often with real-time computing constraints.
Augmented Reality (AR) and Virtual Reality (VR) represent immersive computing technologies that alter or enhance the user's perception of physical and virtual environments.
User Interface (UI) and User Experience (UX) design focus on optimizing the interactive structure and visual elements of a product to make it intuitive and satisfying for users.
Product Management is an organizational function that guides every step of a product's lifecycle: from development, to positioning, pricing, and customer satisfaction.
Quantum Computing is a rapidly-emerging technology that harnesses the laws of quantum mechanics to solve problems too complex for classical computers.
Bioinformatics is an interdisciplinary field that develops methods and software tools for understanding biological data, particularly in genomics and evolutionary biology.
FinTech (Financial Technology) refers to any technology or software used to automate, improve, or deliver financial services and processes for consumers and businesses.
HealthTech (Healthcare Technology) refers to any technology, software, or IT solution designed to improve hospital management, patient care, diagnostics, and medical database security.
